docs.title: Home
docs.text: Welcome to the ROS2_Audio_Tools project wiki.

docs.title: Audio Tools Node implementations
docs.text: Here some brief details as how the functionality is implemented.

docs.title: audio_capture_node
docs.text:
Audio Capture Node using SDL2 for ROS 2

Captures raw audio from an input device and publishes it via ROS topics. Useful for audio processing pipelines, speech recognition, or diagnostics.

docs.title: ✅ Topics
docs.text: Topic Type Description /audio audio_tools/msg/AudioData Raw audio byte stream /audio_stamped audio_tools/msg/AudioDataStamped Audio with timestamp + info /audio_info audio_tools/msg/AudioInfo Metadata (sample rate, format, etc.)

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Default Description sample_format string S16LE Audio format (S16LE, F32LE, etc.) channels int 1 Number of channels (1–8) sample_rate int 16000 Sampling rate in Hz device int -1 Device index (-1 = default input device) chunk_size int 1024 Buffer size per capture call (512–4096)

docs.title: 📦 Message Notes
docs.text:
• AudioData: raw uint8[] data
  • AudioDataStamped:
  • std_msgs/Header header with stamp and frame_id
  • AudioData audio
  • AudioInfo info
  • AudioInfo includes:
  • sample_rate
  • sample_format
  • channels

docs.title: 🧩 Implementation Notes
docs.text:
• Uses SDL2 for low-latency cross-platform audio capture.
  • Device info is printed on startup.
  • Buffer timestamping is calculated based on sample rate and byte depth.
  • Publishes metadata (/audio_info) every 5 seconds.
  • All audio capture runs on a callback triggered by SDL2.

docs.title: 🏁 Launch Example
docs.text:
ros2 run audio_tools audio_capture_node \
  --ros-args \
  -p sample_rate:=16000 \
  -p channels:=1 \
  -p device:=-1

docs.title: 🧪 Dependencies
docs.text:
• rclcpp
  • SDL2
  • Custom messages in audio_tools

docs.title: audio_playback_node
docs.text:
Audio Playback Node using SDL2 for ROS 2

Subscribes to audio messages and plays them in real-time through an output device using SDL2.

docs.title: ✅ Topics
docs.text: Topic Type Description /audio_playback (default) audio_tools/msg/AudioDataStamped Audio stream with metadata

docs.title: ⚙️ Parameters
docs.text: Name Type Default Description audio_topic string /audio_playback Topic to subscribe to for audio data device_index int -1 Index of output device (-1 = default device)

docs.title: 📦 Expected Message Format
docs.text:
• AudioDataStamped:
  • AudioData audio: raw audio byte stream
  • AudioInfo info: playback metadata:
    • sample_rate
    • sample_format
    • channels

docs.title: 🧩 Implementation Notes
docs.text:
• Uses SDL2 to output audio through a selected or default device.
  • Supports multiple formats: S16LE, S16BE, U8, S8, F32LE, F32BE
  • Device info printed on startup.
  • Initializes playback only on receiving the first message (lazy init).
  • Queues audio chunks using SDL_QueueAudio().

docs.title: 🏁 Launch Example
docs.text:
ros2 run audio_tools audio_playback_node \
  --ros-args \
  -p audio_topic:=/audio_stamped \
  -p device_index:=-1

docs.title: 🧪 Dependencies
docs.text:
• rclcpp
  • SDL2
  • Custom messages in audio_tools

docs.title: License
docs.text: This work is licensed under the apache-2.0 license.

docs.title: Contribution rules
docs.text:
For contributing we recommend creating a fork and when ready a pull request to merge the changes with this repository. In the pull request please state:

  • What has been added/changed?
  • Some reasoning about implementation details

Please don't do refactors without consent of administrators as we will not merge them.

docs.title: New features
docs.text: If you have any cool feature/idea to add to the code, please start an issue in GitHub to introduce the idea to the maintainers.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start Examples
docs.text: See node documentation above for parameters & message formats.

docs.title: 🔊 1. Start Audio Capture Node
docs.text:
Capture microphone input and publish to /audio_stamped.

ros2 run audio_tools audio_capture_node

Custom sample rate & stereo:

ros2 run audio_tools audio_capture_node \
  --ros-args -p sample_rate:=44100 -p channels:=2

docs.title: 🎧 2. Start Playback Node (Default Device)
docs.text:
Play back from /audio_stamped using system default output.

ros2 run audio_tools audio_playback_node \
  --ros-args -p audio_topic:=/audio_stamped

docs.title: 🎛️ 3. Use a Specific Output Device
docs.text:
List devices first (done automatically on launch), then select one:

ros2 run audio_tools audio_playback_node \
  --ros-args -p device_index:=1

docs.title: 🔄 4. Full Loopback Test
docs.text:
Run both nodes to capture & play audio:

# Terminal 1
ros2 run audio_tools audio_capture_node

# Terminal 2
ros2 run audio_tools audio_playback_node \
  --ros-args -p audio_topic:=/audio_stamped

docs.title: Getting started with Pixi
docs.text: Pixi makes cross-platform ROS 2 development easy. You can build and run both capture and playback nodes on Linux and Windows—with no system-wide ROS install.

docs.title: 📦 Install Pixi
docs.text:
Linux:

curl -fsSL https://pixi.sh/install.sh | bash

Windows (PowerShell):

powershell -ExecutionPolicy ByPass -c "irm -useb https://pixi.sh/install.ps1 | iex"

docs.title: 🚀 Clone & Build Project
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/CLFML/ROS2_Audio_Tools.git
cd ROS2_Audio_Tools
pixi install
pixi run build

Or launch VSCode with the environment:

pixi run vscode

✅ Note (Windows): Always build in Release or RelWithDebInfo, not Debug! (Ctrl+Shift+P → "CMake: Select Variant")

docs.title: ⚡ Using as a Pixi Dependency
docs.text: Want to use audio_tools from another Pixi-based project?

docs.title: 1. Init a new project
docs.text:
mkdir my_audio_project && cd my_audio_project
pixi init

docs.title: 2. Edit pixi.toml
docs.text:
Add these:

[project]
channels = [
  "https://fast.prefix.dev/conda-forge",
  "https://prefix.dev/robostack-jazzy",
  "https://clfml.github.io/conda_ros2_jazzy_channel/"
]

[dependencies]
ros-jazzy-ros-base = "*"
ros-jazzy-audio-tools = "*"
colcon-common-extensions = "*"
rosdep = "*"

docs.title: 🧠 Optional: VSCode Support
docs.text:
Add to your pixi.toml:

[target.linux-64.dependencies]
python-devtools = "*"
pybind11 = "*"
numpy = "*"

[target.win-64.dependencies]
python-devtools = "*"

[target.linux-64.tasks]
vscode = 'env -u LD_LIBRARY_PATH code .'

[target.win-64.tasks]
vscode = "code ."

docs.title: 3. Run the nodes
docs.text:
pixi install
pixi run ros2 run audio_tools audio_capture_node

docs.title: ▶️ Example: Full Audio Loopback
docs.text:
Launch both nodes using ROS 2 launch (It is really advised to use headphones!):

pixi run build
pixi shell
# Windows:
.\install\setup.bat
# Linux:
source install/setup.sh

docs.title: 🐧 Getting Started with Native ROS
docs.text: Prefer a traditional system-wide install? Use the prebuilt .deb package for Ubuntu Noble / ROS 2 Jazzy.

docs.title: 📦 Install Audio Tools via .deb
docs.text:
Install the latest .deb package directly from Releases:

curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/CLFML/ROS2_Audio_Tools/releases/latest \
  | grep "browser_download_url.*deb" \
  | cut -d : -f 2,3 \
  | tr -d \" \
  | wget -qi -
sudo dpkg -i ./ros-jazzy-audio-tools*.deb

docs.title: ✅ Run the Nodes
docs.text:
Make sure ROS is sourced:

source /opt/ros/jazzy/setup.sh

docs.title: Capture Audio
docs.text: ros2 run audio_tools audio_capture_node

docs.title: Playback Audio
docs.text:
ros2 run audio_tools audio_playback_node \
  --ros-args -p audio_topic:=/audio_stamped

docs.title: 🔁 Full Loopback Example
docs.text:
Start both nodes:

# Terminal 1
ros2 run audio_tools audio_capture_node

# Terminal 2
ros2 run audio_tools audio_playback_node \
  --ros-args -p audio_topic:=/audio_stamped

Let me know if you'd like this wrapped into a script or included in a full install guide!
